description_long: This is a large, round circus-style tent with alternating red and white vertical stripes covering its exterior fabric. The tent features a two-tiered roof, with the upper section being smaller and topped by a golden finial and a yellow flag. The lower section has scalloped black trim along the eaves and a wide, arched entrance at the front, with the fabric pulled back to reveal the interior. Four guy lines extend from the tent's upper and lower edges to the ground, providing structural stability. The tent's fabric appears weathered, with some visible stains and wear, and there are faint decorative patterns on the white panels. The overall design is festive and reminiscent of traditional circus or fairground tents, suitable for parks, plazas, or event spaces in urban environments.
description: A large, round circus-style tent with red and white stripes, a two-tiered roof, a yellow flag, and a wide arched entrance. Guy lines extend from the tent for support, and the fabric shows signs of weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide arched entrance with fabric pulled back, the yellow flag on top, and all four guy lines visible.
description_view_1: Side view with the tent's striped pattern prominent, no visible entrance, and guy lines extending from the sides.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the continuous striped fabric, yellow flag, and guy lines, with no visible entrance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the striped pattern and guy lines, and no entrance visible.
attribute_entrance_type: Arched fabric flap entrance at the front, with fabric pulled back and tied to the sides; no visible zippers or mesh.
attribute_guy_line_configuration: Four guy lines, one extending from each quadrant of the tent, attached at the upper and lower roof edges and staked to the ground.
